title: tanacetum polycephalum subsp. filipendulinum
intro: Tanacetum polycephalum subsp. filipendulinum is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Tanacetum polycephalum plant, also known as the mountain feverfew or polycephalum, which is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and other conditions. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Tanacetum polycephalum subsp. filipendulinum can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
